decision: Drafted docs/brain2daax.md as a pattern-adaptation spec (not a port) covering F1 proxy-secret trust boundary, F2 SBOM both formats, F3 container split, F4 CI gates, F5 RBAC+JIT, F6 SQLi-safe DB console, F7 health, F8 build/provenance page, F9 cloud-agnostic deploy.
rationale: Both repos evaluated from primary sources (novabrain auth.go/dbadmin.go/deploy.sh/version.go; daax lib/auth.ts, config.toml, releases-db.ts). Ranked by leverage/cost; led with F1 because it closes daax backlog task-007 (forward-auth header spoofing). Surfaced SQLite-vs-Postgres as an explicit architectural fork (D1). Added an explicit 'what NOT to port' section per advisor guidance to avoid maximalism.

note: Both reviewers independently verified claims against primary sources; high convergence.

finding: F1 only covered HTTP routes; terminal WS server (4201) authenticates with origin-check only (connection-handler.ts:60) — highest-privilege surface (spawns containers, mounts docker.sock) left unauthenticated. docker:run/start:prod bind TERMINAL_HOST=0.0.0.0, exposing it on the tailnet.
resolution: Split F1 into Part A (HTTP proxy-secret, closes task-007 as scoped) + Part B (authenticate the WS upgrade handshake on every binding; loopback-only in dev bypass). Added §3.1 per-deployment-path reachability table. Re-sequenced F3 to land only after F1 Part B. Added D6 (WS credential model).

finding: JIT insert-detection: 'INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE + changes()' returns 1 for both insert and update in better-sqlite3 — silently re-grants default role to a revoked user, defeating revocation (the bug novabrain's xmax=0 guards).
resolution: Mandated INSERT OR IGNORE + changes()==1 path only; deleted the ON CONFLICT option; wrapped in transaction; added required revoked-user-no-regrant test.

finding: F5 conflated X-Forwarded-User (Pocket ID stable UUID subject) with X-Forwarded-Username (mutable name), and cargo-culted novabrain's email-recycle/OID-change detector which has no purpose under a stable key.
resolution: Decided to key on X-Forwarded-User (stable subject); store username/email as mutable display attrs; explicitly dropped the OID-recycle detector with rationale.

finding: No migration mechanism for the new RBAC schema; Phase 3 skipped it. No backups for rbac.db, no DAAX_PROXY_SECRET rotation, no reconcile/migration rollback.
resolution: Added a PRAGMA user_version migrations runner as a Phase-3 prerequisite (§2). Added §4 Operational Resilience (rbac.db backup/restore, secret rotation with overlap window, reconcile dry-run, migration rollback) gating F1/F5/F6 GA.

finding: F6 multi-DB-file generic write-CRUD console is the maximalism §6 claims to reject; raw writes to rbac.db would bypass app-code auditing; self-referential gating hazard.
resolution: Narrowed F6 to read-first inspection (rbac/audit + list/inspect catalog/releases); env-only super-admin gate disjoint from editable tables; writes opt-in behind a flag and MUST force an auth_audit row. Updated D4 and moved generic write-CRUD to §7 (not-ported).

finding: OCI SBOM attach recommended as default without CI plumbing (id-token: write, cosign, image digest). §8 overstated task-007 closure. Route metric stale (34/128; actually 35/127 files, files != per-method). Dockerfile healthcheck shallow (/) with no /api/health. Runner root vs docker-socket group unreconciled. No testing strategy for auth/RBAC.
resolution: Made workflow-artifact the SBOM default with OCI-attach opt-in + explicit perms (D3). Reworded §9 to 'task-007 closed as scoped' and separated the WS plane. Restated metric as files with unmeasured per-method coverage; wired scripts/audit-auth-routes.ts into CI (F4). F7 notes the shallow healthcheck + missing /api/health. F3 reconciles non-root UID with socket-GID. Added negative-path acceptance tests to F1/F5/F6.

note: Reviewed revised v2; load-bearing factual correction verified by lead against primary sources before applying.

finding: FACTUAL CORRECTION: Traefik template already chains strip-forwarded-headers -> pocket-id-auth on BOTH the /ws and main routers, and compose binds 127.0.0.1:4200/4201. So the compose path is already gated; F1 Part A proxy-secret is belt-and-suspenders there. The genuinely open hole is docker:run / Tailscale-without-Traefik (TERMINAL_HOST=0.0.0.0, -p 4201:4201, no proxy).
resolution: Reframed §0.2, §1.2 auth rows, F1, and §3.1 table. Part A = defense-in-depth for proxy-less ingress only. Part B credential is path-specific: consume X-Forwarded-User on the Traefik /ws route (Traefik forwards identity, NOT the secret); per-connection bearer token for the tailnet/docker:run path.

finding: Tailnet-no-Traefik triple-bind: prescribing 'WS bind loopback in dev' would KILL the supported tailnet-direct mode (auth-gate-001). That mode is exactly where no Traefik-dependent mechanism works.
resolution: Replaced loopback prescription with an in-process per-connection bearer token (HMAC over DAAX_WS_TOKEN_SECRET, short TTL, single-use) via WebSocket subprotocol (NOT URL query — query leaks to logs), keeping tailnet-direct usable AND authenticated. Also fix isAllowedOrigin to reject missing Origin.

finding: Per-connection WS token unspecified across 3 divergent client URL builders (lib/websocket-utils.ts, TerminalManager.tsx, BtopTerminal.tsx which hardcodes :4201 and already breaks the compose loopback path); browser WebSocket cannot set headers; no mint endpoint named.
resolution: Specified /api/terminal/ticket mint endpoint, subprotocol transport, consolidation of the 3 builders, server verifier in connection-handler.ts, and BtopTerminal reconciliation. Added to F1 Part B and D6.

finding: F5 admin reconcile (DAAX_ADMIN_USERS) matches on email/username (the attrs F5 calls untrustworthy/non-key), AND a JIT-only users table means an admin who never logged in cannot be pre-granted -> fresh rbac.db lockout.
resolution: Allow-list accepts subject OR email/username with documented matching (email/username for grant purposes only, never as identity key); reconcile pre-creates a pending grant so first login is authorized. Added to F5.

finding: user_version migration runner collides with existing inline CREATE TABLE on catalog.db/releases.db (already at user_version=0 WITH tables present) — cannot distinguish fresh vs already-populated; 'unified baseline' claim was false.
resolution: Added a baseline-stamp retrofit step (detect existing tables via sqlite_master -> stamp user_version without recreating) before later steps apply; rbac.db starts at v1; dropped the unified-baseline claim until retrofit lands.

finding: F2 SBOM persistence target mismatch: CI/syft runs in GHA disconnected from runtime DBs; only the build route writes releases.db.sbom; image tracking lives in catalog.db.built_images which has vulnerabilities_json but NO sbom column.
resolution: Specified the ingestion path (CI artifact -> release API keyed by image digest) and adding a built_images.sbom_json column via the migration runner so the SBOM is stored against the actual built image the viewer reads.

finding: §4 resilience was a checklist of names: backup-to-daax-data is a no-op in host-dev/docker:run (data dir is cwd/data; volume is compose-only); secret-rotation 'overlap window' contradicted singular DAAX_PROXY_SECRET match; reconcile dry-run had no output contract; migration rollback only said 'restore from backup' (not wired).
resolution: Concretized §4: DAAX_DATA_DIR env-configurable + host-dev rbac.db declared disposable; dual-secret accept (DAAX_PROXY_SECRET + _PREVIOUS) for zero-outage rotation; structured JSON dry-run report contract for reconcile; per-DB pre-migration auto-.backup snapshot hook for rollback.

finding: Admin authz today is NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_MODE — a build-time, client-visible flag (app/settings/page.tsx:89, app/provenance/page.tsx:44); provenance-admin proxy routes are unauthenticated. F5 must replace the flag, not layer over it.
resolution: F5 now explicitly retires NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_MODE for privileged surfaces (route through server-side role check); F4 names the unauthenticated provenance-admin routes as concrete gaps to gate first.

note: Codex-r2 findings #1,#2,#5,#7,#8 (HTTP/WS auth, migration runner, SBOM, CI gates, health not yet implemented) describe CODE state, not spec defects — they confirm the spec's target state is correct and not yet built. No spec change required beyond the specificity already folded in above.

finding: VERDICT CONFLICT (resolved): Opus reviewed the SPEC and confirmed Round-2 fixes are mutually consistent (subprotocol token vs strip-forwarded-headers; dual-secret vs fail-closed validate(); baseline-stamp precedes F2 column add; JIT INSERT OR IGNORE correct) -> SHIP-READY with 5 low items. Codex reviewed CODE-vs-spec and returned NEEDS-ANOTHER-PASS because the controls (proxy-secret, WS auth, migration runner, SBOM ingestion, NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_MODE retirement) are not yet IMPLEMENTED.
resolution: Conflict is about scope of review, not spec quality. Deliverable is a design spec, not implementation; Codex findings #1-7 confirm target state is correct and unbuilt (expected). Accepted Opus SHIP-READY for the document; Codex's implementation gaps become the Phase-1 build backlog. Recorded transparently rather than silently siding with one reviewer.

finding: Factual imprecision: spec attributed TERMINAL_HOST=0.0.0.0 exposure to the docker:run line, but docker:run sets no TERMINAL_HOST; HOST defaults to 'localhost' (constants.ts:12). Exposure is real but via the image CMD start:prod (Dockerfile:148) which sets 0.0.0.0.
resolution: Corrected §0.2 to attribute exposure to the start:prod CMD (+dev:tailscale), noting plain bun dev / CMD-less run stays loopback.

finding: Route-coverage metric weak: '35 of 127 files' is less actionable than the audit's real output. audit-auth-routes.ts reports 39 write-method routes without requireAuth (93 unprotected of 127).
verified: bun run scripts/audit-auth-routes.ts -> 39 unprotected write routes
resolution: Replaced the file-count with the audit's actionable figure (39 write routes unguarded), flagged as point-in-time, reinforcing F4's CI wiring.

finding: Two Phase-1 under-specifications an engineer would have to guess: where the Traefik-path identity read lands, and where single-use ticket state lives.
resolution: F1 Part B now names req.headers['x-forwarded-user'] read inside handleConnection before spawn, and an in-memory jti seen-set in the terminal server (restart-tolerant given short TTL).

decision: 3 rounds x 2 reviewers (Opus + Codex) complete. Spec converged: Round 1 (10 findings, structural), Round 2 (9 findings, factual reframe of the Traefik trust model), Round 3 (5 low-severity polish only). Opus confirms internal consistency and SHIP-READY as a design doc; no new maximalism introduced; §7 exclusions hold. Remaining work is implementation (Phase 1+), tracked as build backlog, not spec defects.
D1 DECIDED by operator: adopt Postgres and CONSOLIDATE all data stores (catalog.db + releases.db + new RBAC/identity/audit) onto it. Overrides the spec's SQLite-first recommendation.
rationale: Operator chose Postgres for maturity and to avoid a hybrid two-engine runtime. Consolidation lets NovaBrain's mechanisms (RETURNING xmax=0 JIT, pg_advisory_xact_lock, information_schema-validated DB console) port directly instead of being re-implemented for SQLite. Cloud path becomes managed Postgres via connection-string swap.
impact: Updated brain2daax.md §2 (decision recorded; migration tooling = Node pg migration tool; one-time SQLite->PG data migration with parity tests), F2 (built_images.sbom_json jsonb), F5 (Postgres schema + xmax=0 JIT + advisory-lock reconcile), F6 (information_schema validation, single DB), §4 (pg_dump/snapshot backups, down-migrations), §6 Phase 0 (stand up Postgres + migrate), §7 (Postgres machinery now adopted), §8 (Postgres container local / managed cloud), §9, §10 D1 marked decided.

decision: Create GitHub issues grouped by phase (not review round — rounds were iterations). One issue per feature + an epic, all as DRAFT (repo's approval-gate label) under Phase 0-4 milestones, each with explicit acceptance criteria + required unit/integration/E2E testing.

note: All DRAFT pending JP approval. Each issue has explicit acceptance criteria + required unit/integration/E2E testing + both-deploy-mode checks. Key edge: #95 must precede #100.

decision: Adopted node-pg-migrate as daax-web's Postgres migration tool. Added lib/db/config.ts (env->connection resolver, fail-closed), lib/db/pg.ts (lazy pg.Pool singleton consuming the same config), a baseline migration (migrations/1781568000000_baseline-schema-meta.js creating schema_meta), scripts/db-migrate.ts runner, and postgres + one-shot migrate services in BOTH docker-compose.yml and deploy/docker-compose.yml (persistent named volume daax-pg-data, pg_isready healthcheck, app gated on migrate service_completed_successfully).
rationale: node-pg-migrate chosen over Drizzle (schema-first ORM — heavier than an empty Phase-0 baseline needs; the data-layer rewrite is #migrate's job, not Phase 0) and Atlas (Go binary — violates the issue's 'Node migration tool' requirement, adds a non-Node runtime dep). node-pg-migrate is pure Node, ships ordered timestamped up/down migrations, tracks applied state in the pgmigrations table (idempotent re-run), runs in a single transaction by default, and exposes a programmatic runner so the runtime pool and the migration runner share one env-sourced connection path. App boot is intentionally NOT coupled to Postgres (one-shot compose migrate service, not the app CMD) so docker:run without compose still starts; domain tables land in #migrate and Phase 3.

finding: docker compose end-to-end (run once Docker daemon was available) surfaced a real bug the local-postgres-binary test could not: postgres:18 stores PGDATA under a version subdir and the image REFUSES to start when the data volume is mounted at /var/lib/postgresql/data (PGDATA would land off-volume and not persist).
resolution: Changed the daax-pg-data mount target from /var/lib/postgresql/data to /var/lib/postgresql in BOTH compose files so PGDATA (/var/lib/postgresql/18/docker) lands on the volume.
verified: docker build OK (migrations/ + scripts/ + node-pg-migrate baked into the image); local & deploy compose: postgres healthy, one-shot migrate APPLIES the baseline (exit 0), idempotent re-run = no-op, schema_meta survives 'docker compose restart postgres' AND 'docker compose down' (keep-volume) + 'up', deploy mode fail-closed without DAAX_PG_PASSWORD.

decision: Ported catalog.db + releases.db to Postgres. Added migrations/1781568000100_catalog-releases-schema.js (10 tables, jsonb for JSON-as-text, bigserial for AUTOINCREMENT PKs, timestamptz). Rewrote lib/catalog/db.ts and lib/releases-db.ts against the pg pool (async); updated all API call sites to await. Added scripts/export-sqlite-to-postgres.ts (one-shot exporter, db:export) with ON CONFLICT idempotency + sequence resync. Moved better-sqlite3 to devDependencies (exporter-only; no runtime import).
design: jsonb columns: write JSON.stringify (node-pg renders bare arrays as PG array literals, wrong for jsonb), read parsed objects. releases.feature_config/sbom keep their public string contract via stringify-on-read. timestamptz normalised to ISO strings on read. backupDatabase reimplemented as a dependency-free logical JSON export (engine-level pg_dump/snapshot policy is #103). built_images FK to build_specs/build_jobs is ON DELETE SET NULL (retain the built image, null its linkage when a spec/job is deleted).
verified: typecheck/lint(0 errors)/format/host build clean; full unit suite 1040 passed; integration suite (real PG) 10 passed incl. CRUD + exporter parity (per-table row counts + cross-engine content checksums match). HOST mode (next start + DATABASE_URL) and CONTAINER mode (docker build + isolated postgres:18 container) both serve /api/catalog/bases (8 seeded), /api/catalog/features (14), and POST/GET /api/releases from Postgres with feature_config string contract preserved. Playwright spec tests/e2e/catalog-releases.spec.ts added (skips without PG).
